event.name: 1913 US Open
event.stakes: Playoff against Vardon and Ray. A 20-year-old caddie-turned-amateur against the two best players in the world.

conditions.weather: Wet. Fog in the morning. Rain had softened the course.
conditions.surface: The Country Club, Brookline — short, tight, unforgiving

gear.note: Made in St Andrews. The pipe trademark stamped on the back of each head. Hickory, not steel — you felt every shot through the wood grain. A mashie-niblick. A mid-iron. A niblick. Tools from another century already.

moment: The 17th hole of the playoff. A 15-foot putt to go ahead of Vardon and Ray. His 10-year-old caddie Eddie Lowery told him before every putt that day: 'Keep your eye on the ball.'
note: He was a caddie who became a champion. Nothing in the bag was remarkable. The round was.

event.name: 1960 US Open
event.stakes: Seven shots back at the start of the round. A sportswriter told him at lunch he had no chance.

conditions.weather: Hot. Thin Denver air. The ball carrying farther than anywhere else.
conditions.surface: Cherry Hills CC — 346-yard downhill par-4 opening hole

gear.note: The original is still locked in the Cherry Hills clubhouse. He drove it through the thin altitude air onto the first green — one hop through the rough, rolled to the putting surface.
The blade that accounted for 14 wins in two years. Heavy, honest, no forgiveness built in. He'd used them since turning pro.

moment: First hole, first shot of the final round. He drove the green on the par-4 1st. Made birdie. Then five more in the first seven holes. Seven back to start. Champion by the end.

event.name: 1986 Masters
event.stakes: 46 years old. The writers had already written the obituary for his career.

conditions.weather: Clear. Augusta running fast. Sunday afternoon on the back nine.
conditions.surface: Augusta National — firm, quick, no margin

gear.note: Aluminum head. He painted it black himself. Loaded the rear cavity with lead tape to give it feel. When his designer first showed it to him, Nicklaus asked: 'Is this a joke?' It wasn't. Nobody knows where it is now — one of his sons gave it away.

moment: The back nine. 17th — a downhill left-to-lefter, made it. 18 — eagle attempt rolled to six feet, made birdie. Six birdies and an eagle from the 9th hole on. Final round 65.
note: The putter was a joke until it wasn't. That's most of it.

event.name: 1991 PGA Championship
event.stakes: The 9th alternate. Got the call at midnight. Never saw the course before the first tee Monday morning.

conditions.weather: August heat in Indiana. Humid. Course playing long and tight.
conditions.surface: Crooked Stick GC — narrow fairways, not built for what was about to happen

gear.note: White, plastic-looking head. Titanium shaft. His playing partner Bob Lohr had never seen anything like it. It went places Crooked Stick had never been hit before.

moment: Every tee box, all four days. He didn't lay up. He didn't know the course. He hit it 350 yards and figured out the rest from there. Won by three.
note: The 9th alternate hit it past everyone and waited for the course to catch up.

event.name: 2004 Masters
event.stakes: Forty-two professional major starts without a win. One shot ahead of Ernie Els on 18.

conditions.weather: Warm. Sunday afternoon. Augusta moving fast.

gear.note: His last major with Titleist before moving to Callaway. One last putt with the clubs he'd carried through forty-two attempts.

moment: 18th green. An 18-foot birdie putt. The whole story of his career had been building to something exactly like this. He made it. Fist pump before it dropped.
note: Forty-two starts without one. Then this.

event.name: 2019 Masters
event.stakes: Leading group. Final round. Eleven years since his last major.

conditions.weather: Firm greens. Pins tucked. Air heavy.

gear.note: Worn in. Not the newest version. The one he trusts.
Built to his exact specs — offset, trajectory, tungsten placement. Familiar, not forgiving. Kept the ball down.

moment: 16 — center of the green. No flag hunting. Let the course take the others.
note: Didn't chase it. Played what he could hold.

event.name: 2011 US Open
event.stakes: Eight shots clear. Record on the table.

conditions.surface: Congressional CC — set up for scoring

gear.note: Hit every fairway he needed. Not because he was careful — because he was dialed.
Blades. Pure contact all week. He didn't need forgiveness.

moment: The whole week, really. Congressional bent toward him. He didn't press — just kept playing the shot in front of him. Finished at 16-under. Eight clear.
note: Some weeks the swing is so far ahead of the doubt that there's nothing to manage.

conditions.weather: Cold hands. Wet fairways. Leaves everywhere.

gear.note: Mixed range balls in the bag. Glove stiff by the turn. Didn't matter.

moment: Short par 4 — laid up instead of forcing driver. Wedge in. Two putts.
note: Stopped trying to prove something. Played the day he had.
